mnemonics: LoRaWAN: Long RAnge Wide Area Network - think of it as a 'Long Range Whispering ANtenna' for devices to talk quietly over miles.
keyPoints: Uses unlicensed radio spectrum (e.g., 868 MHz in EU, 915 MHz in US) for low-power, wide-area communication.
Employs an Adaptive Data Rate (ADR) algorithm to optimize data rate, airtime, and power consumption.
Operates in a star-of-stars topology where end devices communicate with gateways, which forward data to a central network server.
commonMistakes: Confusing LoRa (the physical layer/chip protocol) with LoRaWAN (the MAC layer/network architecture).
Assuming it is suitable for high-bandwidth applications like video streaming, when it is designed for small, infrequent data packets.
